name: Skating
overview: This merit badge introduces Scouts to the exciting world of ice skating, roller skating, in-line skating, and skateboarding. In addition to being an enjoyable leisure activity that promotes fitness, there are a variety of athletic careers in skating.

content.value: Do the following:

sub_requirements.label: a
sub_requirements.text: Explain to your counselor the most likely hazards associated with skating and what you should do to anticipate, help prevent, mitigate, and respond to these hazards.

content.value: Explain to your counselor the most likely hazards associated with skating and what you should do to anticipate, help prevent, mitigate, and respond to these hazards.

sub_requirements.label: b
sub_requirements.text: Show that you know first aid for injuries or illnesses that could occur while skating, including hypothermia, frostbite, lacerations, abrasions, fractures, sprains and strains, concussions, blisters, heat-related reactions, and shock.

content.value: Show that you know first aid for injuries or illnesses that could occur while skating, including hypothermia, frostbite, lacerations, abrasions, fractures, sprains and strains, concussions, blisters, heat-related reactions, and shock.

requirements.text: Working under the supervision of an experienced adult, do ONE of the following options.

content.value: Working under the supervision of an experienced adult, do ONE of the following options.

sub_requirements.text: Option A—Ice Skating. Do ALL of the following:

content.value: Do ALL of the following:

sub_requirements.text: Explain the general safety and courtesy guidelines for ice skating.

content.value: Explain the general safety and courtesy guidelines for ice skating.

sub_requirements.text: Discuss preparations that must be taken when skating outdoors on natural ice. Explain how to make an ice rescue.

content.value: Discuss preparations that must be taken when skating outdoors on natural ice. Explain how to make an ice rescue.

resources.title: How To Know if Ice Is Safe To Walk On (video)
How to Save Someone Who Falls Through Ice (video)

sub_requirements.text: Discuss the parts and functions of the different types of ice skates.

content.value: Discuss the parts and functions of the different types of ice skates.

sub_requirements.text: Describe the proper way to carry ice skates at the rink and for travel.

content.value: Describe the proper way to carry ice skates at the rink and for travel.

sub_requirements.text: Describe how to store ice skates for long periods of time, such as seasonal storage.

content.value: Describe how to store ice skates for long periods of time, such as seasonal storage.

resources.title: How Do I Store My Skates Correctly? (video)

sub_requirements.text: Skate forward at least 40 feet and come to a complete stop. Use either a two-foot snowplow stop or a one-foot snowplow stop.

content.value: Skate forward at least 40 feet and come to a complete stop. Use either a two-foot snowplow stop or a one-foot snowplow stop.

sub_requirements.text: After skating forward, glide forward on two feet, then on one foot, first right and then left.

content.value: After skating forward, glide forward on two feet, then on one foot, first right and then left.

sub_requirements.text: Starting from a T position, stroke forward around the test area, avoiding the use of toe picks if wearing figure skates.

content.value: Starting from a T position, stroke forward around the test area, avoiding the use of toe picks if wearing figure skates.

sub_requirements.text: Glide backward on two feet for at least two times the skater's height.

content.value: Glide backward on two feet for at least two times the skater's height.

sub_requirements.text: Skate backward for at least 20 feet on two skates.

content.value: Skate backward for at least 20 feet on two skates.

sub_requirements.text: After gaining forward speed, glide forward on two feet, making a turn of 180 degrees around a cone, first to the right and then to the left.

content.value: After gaining forward speed, glide forward on two feet, making a turn of 180 degrees around a cone, first to the right and then to the left.

sub_requirements.text: Perform forward crossovers in a figure-eight pattern.

content.value: Perform forward crossovers in a figure-eight pattern.

resources.title: How To Do Back Crossovers in a Figure 8 (video)

sub_requirements.text: Explain to your counselor the safety considerations for participating in an ice-skating race.

content.value: Explain to your counselor the safety considerations for participating in an ice-skating race.

resources.title: How Speed Skaters Stay Safe on the Ice (website)

sub_requirements.text: Perform a hockey stop.

content.value: Perform a hockey stop.

sub_requirements.text: Option B—Roller Skating. Do ALL of the following:

content.value: Do ALL of the following:

sub_requirements.text: Explain the general safety rules and etiquette guidelines for roller skating.

content.value: Explain the general safety rules and etiquette guidelines for roller skating.

sub_requirements.text: Discuss the parts and functions of a roller skate and their functions.

content.value: Discuss the parts and functions of a roller skate and their functions.

sub_requirements.text: Describe five essential steps to good roller skate care.

content.value: Describe five essential steps to good roller skate care.

sub_requirements.text: Skate forward with smooth, linked strokes on two feet for at least 100 feet in both directions around the rink and demonstrate proper techniques for stopping.

content.value: Skate forward with smooth, linked strokes on two feet for at least 100 feet in both directions around the rink and demonstrate proper techniques for stopping.

sub_requirements.text: Skate forward and glide at least 15 feet on one skate, then on the other skate.

content.value: Skate forward and glide at least 15 feet on one skate, then on the other skate.

sub_requirements.text: Perform a forward crossover.

content.value: Perform a forward crossover.

sub_requirements.text: Skate backward for at least 40 feet on two skates, then for at least 15 feet on one skate.

content.value: Skate backward for at least 40 feet on two skates, then for at least 15 feet on one skate.

sub_requirements.text: Skate forward in a slalom pattern for at least 40 feet on two skates, then for at least 20 feet on one skate.

content.value: Skate forward in a slalom pattern for at least 40 feet on two skates, then for at least 20 feet on one skate.

sub_requirements.text: Skate backward in a slalom pattern for at least 15 feet on two skates.

content.value: Skate backward in a slalom pattern for at least 15 feet on two skates.

sub_requirements.text: Shuttle skate once around the rink, bending twice along the way, without stopping.

content.value: Shuttle skate once around the rink, bending twice along the way, without stopping.

sub_requirements.text: Perform a series of two consecutive spins on skates, OR hop, skip, and jump on skates for at least 10 feet.

content.value: Perform a series of two consecutive spins on skates, OR hop, skip, and jump on skates for at least 10 feet.

sub_requirements.text: Perform a stepover.

content.value: Perform a stepover.

sub_requirements.text: While skating, dribble a basketball at least 40 feet, then back to your starting position, OR push a hockey ball with a stick at least 40 feet, then back to your starting position.

content.value: While skating, dribble a basketball at least 40 feet, then back to your starting position, OR push a hockey ball with a stick at least 40 feet, then back to your starting position.

sub_requirements.text: Option C—In-Line Skating. Do ALL of the following:

content.value: Do ALL of the following:

sub_requirements.text: Explain the general safety rules and courtesy guidelines for in-line skating.

content.value: Explain the general safety rules and courtesy guidelines for in-line skating.

sub_requirements.text: Describe the parts of an in-line skate and their functions.

content.value: Describe the parts of an in-line skate and their functions.

resources.title: Types and Parts of Inline Skates (video)

sub_requirements.text: Describe the required and recommended safety equipment for in-line skating.

content.value: Describe the required and recommended safety equipment for in-line skating.

sub_requirements.text: Explain four essential steps to good in-line skate care.

content.value: Explain four essential steps to good in-line skate care.

resources.title: Maintaining and Caring for Your In-Line Skates (video)

sub_requirements.text: Skate forward with smooth, linked strokes on two feet for at least 100 feet.

content.value: Skate forward with smooth, linked strokes on two feet for at least 100 feet.

sub_requirements.text: Skate forward and glide at least 15 feet on one skate, then on the other skate.

content.value: Skate forward and glide at least 15 feet on one skate, then on the other skate.

sub_requirements.text: Stop on command on flat pavement using the heel brake.

content.value: Stop on command on flat pavement using the heel brake.

sub_requirements.text: Perform a forward crossover.

content.value: Perform a forward crossover.

resources.title: How to Turn on In-Line Skates (video)

sub_requirements.text: Perform a series of forward, linked swizzles for at least 40 feet.

content.value: Perform a series of forward, linked swizzles for at least 40 feet.

sub_requirements.text: Skate backward for at least 40 feet in a series of linked, backward swizzles.

content.value: Skate backward for at least 40 feet in a series of linked, backward swizzles.

sub_requirements.text: From a strong pace, perform a lunge turn around an object predetermined by your counselor.

content.value: From a strong pace, perform a lunge turn around an object predetermined by your counselor.

sub_requirements.text: Perform a mohawk.

content.value: Perform a mohawk.

sub_requirements.text: Perform a series of at least four one-footed downhill slaloms on pavement with a gentle slope.

content.value: Perform a series of at least four one-footed downhill slaloms on pavement with a gentle slope.

sub_requirements.text: Describe how to pass a pedestrian or another skater from behind.

content.value: Describe how to pass a pedestrian or another skater from behind.

sub_requirements.text: Describe at least three ways to avoid an unforeseen obstacle while skating.

content.value: Describe at least three ways to avoid an unforeseen obstacle while skating.

sub_requirements.text: Describe two ways to get on and off a curb, and demonstrate at least one of these methods.

content.value: Describe two ways to get on and off a curb, and demonstrate at least one of these methods.

sub_requirements.text: Option D—Skateboarding. Do ALL of the following:

content.value: Do ALL of the following:

sub_requirements.text: Explain the history and evolution of skateboarding.

content.value: Explain the history and evolution of skateboarding.

resources.title: How Has Skateboarding Changed Over the Years (video)

sub_requirements.text: Describe the benefits of skateboarding (physical fitness, balance, coordination, perseverance, and creativity)

content.value: Describe the benefits of skateboarding (physical fitness, balance, coordination, perseverance, and creativity)

sub_requirements.text: Explain the safety rules and courtesy guidelines for skateboarding, including right-of-way and communication signals and warnings.

content.value: Explain the safety rules and courtesy guidelines for skateboarding, including right-of-way and communication signals and warnings.

resources.title: How to Ride Skateparks for Beginners (video)
How to Ride Safely on a Skateboard (video)

sub_requirements.text: Explain the protective gear needed to skate safely, including helmet, knee pads, elbow pads, and wrist guards, and show how to wear them correctly.

content.value: Explain the protective gear needed to skate safely, including helmet, knee pads, elbow pads, and wrist guards, and show how to wear them correctly.

sub_requirements.text: Describe the two main styles of skateboarding (street and vert), and describe common skatepark features (ramps, manual pad, rails, spine, etc.).

content.value: Describe the two main styles of skateboarding (street and vert), and describe common skatepark features (ramps, manual pad, rails, spine, etc.).

sub_requirements.text: Explain skateboard anatomy (deck, trucks, wheels, hardware, etc.).

content.value: Explain skateboard anatomy (deck, trucks, wheels, hardware, etc.).

sub_requirements.text: Build a board by assembling all pieces (deck, wheels, trucks, hardware, and grip tape) in the proper order.

content.value: Build a board by assembling all pieces (deck, wheels, trucks, hardware, and grip tape) in the proper order.

sub_requirements.text: Explain skateboard maintenance (cleaning, adjusting bolts, and replacing parts).

content.value: Explain skateboard maintenance (cleaning, adjusting bolts, and replacing parts).

sub_requirements.text: Explain the difference between regular and goofy stance, and demonstrate your preferred stance.

content.value: Explain the difference between regular and goofy stance, and demonstrate your preferred stance.

sub_requirements.text: Demonstrate how to push, cruise, and carve on flat ground.

content.value: Demonstrate how to push, cruise, and carve on flat ground.

resources.title: How to Push on a Skateboard (video)
How to Turn on a Skateboard (video)

sub_requirements.text: Show at least two braking and stopping techniques.

content.value: Show at least two braking and stopping techniques.

sub_requirements.text: Do a basic ollie and at least one variation.

content.value: Do a basic ollie and at least one variation.

sub_requirements.text: Show how to drop-in from coping.

content.value: Show how to drop-in from coping.

sub_requirements.text: Demonstrate THREE skateboarding tricks from the following types:

content.value: Demonstrate THREE skateboarding tricks from the following types:

sub_requirements.label: a
sub_requirements.text: Flat ground tricks

content.value: Flat ground tricks

sub_requirements.label: b
sub_requirements.text: Flip and shove-it tricks

content.value: Flip and shove-it tricks

sub_requirements.label: c
sub_requirements.text: Grind and slide tricks

content.value: Grind and slide tricks

sub_requirements.label: d
sub_requirements.text: Air and grab tricks

content.value: Air and grab tricks

sub_requirements.label: e
sub_requirements.text: Footplant tricks.

content.value: Footplant tricks.
